summary: FT Common decompilation slice touching common fighter source files, led by a large rewrite/matching pass in ftCo_0A01.c plus smaller improvements across damage, down-bound, item throw, warp star, and related files. The PR body reports a clean ninja changes_all/regression gate, while bot output reports 2 new matches and 29 unmatched-item improvements. One author review comment flags newly added float externs in ftCo_0A01.c as likely needing removal and inlining.

key_files.role: primary large matching/decompilation target; 661 added, 434 deleted, 82 hunks; bot reported .data match and many function improvements
matching cleanup in ftCo_8009F834, including local renames and scoped joint temporaries
common fighter damage matching improvements; bot reported gains in ftCo_8008EB58, ftCo_8008DCE0, and ftCo_8008EC90
down-bound matching improvements; bot reported gains in ftCo_80097AF4, ftCo_800976A4, and ftCo_8009794C
item-throw matching improvements; bot reported gains in ftCo_80095EFC and ftCo_80095D5C
warp-star matching target; bot reported ftCo_800C4C60 reached 100%
small common fighter update; bot reported slight ftCo_800C08A0 improvement
small damage-ice matching improvements; bot reported gains in ftCo_DamageIce_HitWhileFrozen and ftCo_DamageIce_Init
header surface changed by one-line edit

what_changed: PR body identifies the work as an FT common decompilation slice rebased onto origin/master f59b5e08b5.
Changed-file metadata lists 11 changed files under src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon, with ftCo_0A01.c carrying most churn.
Bot report shows matched code +460 bytes and matched data +840 bytes, with 2 new matches: ftCo_0A01 .data and ftCo_WarpStar ftCo_800C4C60.
Bot report lists 29 improvements in unmatched items, heavily concentrated in ftCo_0A01 functions and also covering Damage, DownBound, ItemThrow, DamageIce, 09F7, and Bury units.

Diff excerpt for ftCo_09F7.c renames register/temp-style locals to role names such as fp, part, scale_y, z_range, floor_angle, and random_or_angle, while preserving the goto/block-shaped control flow.
Diff excerpt for ftCo_09F7.c introduces narrow HSD_JObj* joint temporaries around some efAsync_Spawn calls.

Diff excerpt for ftCo_0A01.c changes control-flow/source shape in functions such as ftCo_800A229C, ftCo_800A2718, and ftCo_800A2C80, including if-chain shaping, scoped local blocks, declaration/order changes, and PAD_STACK/pragmas retained in shown excerpts.
A review comment on ftCo_0A01.c flags added extern const f32 declarations at addresses 4D87D8, 4D8808, and 4D8870 as likely needing removal and inlining.

smart_moves.text: The PR body records applying the slice alone onto the production baseline and running ninja changes_all with a completed no-regression report, which gives the matching changes explicit verification context.
The ftCo_09F7.c excerpt improves readability by replacing temp_r30/var_r27-style locals with role names fp and part when the roles are clear.
The PR kept an out-of-slice dashboard lint finding in gm_18A5.c separate from this slice rather than implying the FT Common changes caused it.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For fighter common code, local role names like fp for Fighter* and part for enum Fighter_Part are useful replacements for decompiler temp/register names once the role is evident.
Float constants exposed as address-named externs should be treated cautiously; this PR contains author feedback that newly added const f32 externs likely need to be removed and inlined somewhere.

Large FT Common matching slices may rely on declaration order, scoped temporaries, and localized source-shape changes to improve matching, but the PR evidence does not establish these as general style; they should remain evidence-backed matching tactics.

naming_conventions.convention: Use fp for a clear Fighter* local and part for a clear enum Fighter_Part local instead of temp_rXX/var_rXX names.
Use descriptive float locals for locally understood values, as shown by scale_y, z_range, floor_angle, and random_or_angle replacing generic temp/var float names.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Scoped temporaries can be introduced around specific calls to influence local codegen while limiting lifetime, as shown by block-scoped HSD_JObj* joint variables before efAsync_Spawn calls.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The PR slice shows the tactic but does not include per-hunk objdiff explanation; keep narrow and verify.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Declaration ordering and assignment ordering were adjusted in ftCo_0A01.c functions such as ftCo_800A2C80, including splitting vector component math into v, vy, x_sq, y_sq, and is_small locals.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: Treat as matching-specific source shape rather than global style.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Some generated-looking control flow was retained or reshaped into if/goto forms rather than fully structured switches, notably in ftCo_800A229C and ftCo_800A2718.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: Loaded evidence supports that this occurred, not that it is generally preferred.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK and #pragma dont_inline appear in the changed excerpts, so any retained uses should be considered evidence-required matching tactics under current standards.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The PR body gives whole-slice build/regression evidence but not a local justification for every tactic.

review_feedback.author: fjooord
review_feedback.comment: likely need to remove these externs and inline somewhere
review_feedback.subject: new extern const f32 ftCo_804D87D8, ftCo_804D8808, and ftCo_804D8870 declarations

follow_up_queries: ftCo_804D87D8 ftCo_804D8808 ftCo_804D8870 extern const f32 inline
PR 2724 ftCommon regression report no regressions

notes: PR metadata, body, changed-file list, bot report, and review comment are loaded and consistent for the main 11 changed files.
The PR body lists src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_ThrownKirby.c, but changed_files metadata/jsonl does not list it among the 11 changed files; it is not treated as changed in this postmortem.
The raw diff is truncated in the loaded slice, so file-specific claims are limited to shown excerpts and bot summary rows.
The PR body reports build/regression verification; this postmortem records that report but does not independently validate builds.
Only one review comment is loaded, authored by the PR author, so broader reviewer intent or acceptance rationale is not inferred.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Clear fighter locals may use fp and part instead of temp_rXX/var_rXX
accepted_candidate_records.text: In ftCo_09F7.c, Fighter* temp_r30 was renamed to fp and enum Fighter_Part var_r27 to part throughout ftCo_8009F834, improving readability where the roles were clear.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Address-named float externs are suspect without data ownership evidence
accepted_candidate_records.text: A PR review comment on ftCo_0A01.c says newly added extern const f32 declarations for ftCo_804D87D8, ftCo_804D8808, and ftCo_804D8870 likely need removal and inlining.

source_update_candidates.title: Use PR 2724 as an example for keeping ordinary float constants inline instead of adding address-named externs
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2724 contains an author review comment on ftCo_0A01.c: newly added extern const f32 ftCo_804D87D8, ftCo_804D8808, and ftCo_804D8870 likely need to be removed and inlined somewhere. This could serve as an example under literals-and-data-ownership, not as a new standard.
source_update_candidates.reason: The feedback directly aligns with the existing standard against promoting literals into named storage without ownership evidence.

source_update_candidates.title: ftCommon matching slices often touch many small action files plus ftCo_0A01.c
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2724's changed-file metadata shows a large ftCo_0A01.c matching pass plus smaller edits to ftCo_09F7.c, ftCo_Bury.c, ftCo_Damage.c, ftCo_DamageIce.c, ftCo_DownBound.c, ftCo_Guard.c, ftCo_ItemThrow.c, ftCo_Shouldered.c, and ftCo_WarpStar.c.
source_update_candidates.reason: May help future path-scoped context, but should be curated because one PR body file listing disagrees with changed-file metadata.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the if/goto control-flow shapes in ftCo_0A01.c as preferred authored style; the PR evidence only shows they were used in this matching slice.
Do not promote PAD_STACK or #pragma dont_inline uses as normal style; loaded standards require narrow evidence for such tactics.
Do not treat ftCo_ThrownKirby.c as changed in this PR despite appearing in the PR body file list, because changed_files metadata and jsonl omit it.
Do not infer that the flagged float externs were actually fixed in this PR; the loaded evidence only contains the comment.
